prompt: Integrator: build the agentic eval verdict that ties the three parts together. You score only held-out items, because a contaminated item measures memorization, not skill. A task passes only when the tool call was parsed correctly by the template AND the agent was actually capable on the task. Given a list of task results, each with parsed_ok, capability_ok, and held_out, compute the observed pass rate and the true capability rate over the held-out items, then diagnose whether a low score points to a template problem or a real capability gap. Fill the two TODO helpers, then compose them.
starter_code:
def held_out_only(results):
    # Keep only the items that were held out of training.
    # TODO: return the list of results where held_out is True.
    pass


def rate(results, predicate):
    # Fraction of results where predicate(item) is True. 0.0 for an empty list.
    # TODO
    pass


def eval_verdict(results, gap_threshold):
    scored = held_out_only(results)
    observed = rate(scored, lambda r: r["parsed_ok"] and r["capability_ok"])
    capability = rate(scored, lambda r: r["capability_ok"])
    gap = capability - observed
    return {
        "scored": len(scored),
        "observed_rate": round(observed, 2),
        "capability_rate": round(capability, 2),
        "template_gap": round(gap, 2),
        "diagnosis": "template" if gap >= gap_threshold else "capability",
    }
constraints: Score only held-out items, because a contaminated item measures memorization and inflates the result.
A task passes only when the template parsed the call correctly and the agent was capable, so both must be true.
A large gap between the capability rate and the observed rate points to a template problem, not a weak model.

walkthrough.title: From raw results to a template-versus-capability verdict

steps.title: Keep only held-out items
steps.detail: held_out_only drops items that leaked into training, so the score reflects generalization rather than memorization.

steps.title: Measure two rates
steps.detail: observed pass requires parsed_ok and capability_ok together, while the capability rate looks at capability_ok alone, which isolates the template effect.
steps.output: an observed rate and a capability rate

steps.title: Diagnose the gap
steps.detail: eval_verdict reports both rates and diagnoses a template problem when the capability rate sits well above the observed rate.

io_examples.label: template problem
io_examples.explanation: Every task was within capability, but two calls failed to parse, so the observed rate is 0.5 while capability is 1.0, and the 0.5 gap flags a template problem.

io_examples.label: capability gap
io_examples.explanation: The template parsed every call, so the observed rate equals the capability rate and the zero gap points to a real capability gap.

visualization.title: Held-out, two rates, verdict
visualization.description: How held-out results become a template or capability diagnosis.

items.label: observed vs capability rate
items.value: parsed and capable versus capable alone

items.label: diagnosis
items.value: template gap or real capability gap

worked_examples.label: basic
worked_examples.title: Explicit helpers
worked_examples.explanation: held_out_only filters the items, rate computes a guarded fraction, and eval_verdict compares the two rates to diagnose the gap.

worked_examples.label: concise
worked_examples.title: Idiomatic Python
worked_examples.code:
def eval_verdict(results, gap_threshold):
    scored = [r for r in results if r["held_out"]]
    n = len(scored)
    observed = (sum(1 for r in scored if r["parsed_ok"] and r["capability_ok"]) / n) if n else 0.0
    capability = (sum(1 for r in scored if r["capability_ok"]) / n) if n else 0.0
    gap = capability - observed
    return {"scored": n, "observed_rate": round(observed, 2), "capability_rate": round(capability, 2), "template_gap": round(gap, 2), "diagnosis": "template" if gap >= gap_threshold else "capability"}
worked_examples.explanation: The held-out filter and both rates are inlined, and the diagnosis is the comparison of the gap to the threshold.

hints.text: First keep only the held-out items, because contaminated items would inflate the score.
held_out_only returns the results where held_out is True.
rate divides the count that match a predicate by the total, with a guard for the empty list.
observed uses parsed_ok and capability_ok together, capability uses capability_ok alone.
diagnosis is template when the gap is at least the threshold, otherwise capability.

tests.description: capable model blocked by parsing is a template problem
no parse gap means a real capability gap
contaminated items are excluded from scoring

hidden_tests.description: no held-out items yields a zero observed rate
